name: console-setup
title: console-setup
summary: console font and keymap setup program
description:
console font and keymap setup program
This package provides the console with the same keyboard
configuration scheme as the X Window System. As a result, there is no
need to duplicate or change the keyboard files just to make simple
customizations such as the use of dead keys, the key functioning as
AltGr or Compose key, the key(s) to switch between Latin and
non-Latin mode, etc.
.
The package also installs console fonts supporting many of the
world's languages.  It provides an unified set of font faces - the
classic VGA, the simplistic Fixed, and the cleaned Terminus,
TerminusBold and TerminusBoldVGA.

commands.name: ckbcomp
commands.description: compile a XKB keyboard description to a keymap suitable for loadkeys or kbdcontrol

arguments.description: Specifies the behaviour of the <BackSpace> and <Delete> keys. Value bs specifies
arguments.raw: -backspace  Specifies the behaviour of the <BackSpace> and <Delete> keys. Value bs specifies

arguments.description: Generate a compact keymap with at most two xkb groups and two levels in each or only
arguments.raw: -compact  Generate a compact keymap with at most two xkb groups and two levels in each or only

arguments.description: Look in the top-level directory dir for files included by the keymap description. This
arguments.raw: -Idir  Look in the top-level directory dir for files included by the keymap description. This

commands.name: setupcon
commands.description: sets up the font and the keyboard on the console

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ckbcomp because console font and keymap setup program.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ckbcomp as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ckbcomp [OPTION...] [XKBLAYOUT [XKBVARIANT [XKBOPTIONS]...]]` as documented upstream for ckbcomp.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ckbcomp with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ckbcomp --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install console-setup. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `ckbcomp [OPTION...] [XKBLAYOUT [XKBVARIANT [XKBOPTIONS]...]]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: console font and keymap setup program.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: ckbcomp - compile a XKB keyboard description to a keymap suitable for loadkeys or kbdcontrol.
